title: What is BrowsePad?
content: BrowsePad is a TV-first web browser built for Android TV and Google TV devices. It is designed for large-screen browsing with a desktop-style browser interface, multi-tab support, TV-remote navigation, and an optional phone-based trackpad controller called BrowsePad Connect.

title: What devices does BrowsePad work on?
content: BrowsePad is designed for Android TV and Google TV devices that support the Leanback TV experience. It is optimized for televisions, streaming boxes, and other TV form-factor devices that use a D-pad remote, and it also supports pairing with a phone over the same local network for easier pointer control.

title: Can I use BrowsePad with just my TV remote?
content: Yes. BrowsePad is fully usable with a normal TV remote. You can navigate using the D-pad, use the center or OK button to click, hold directional buttons for continuous pointer movement, and long-press OK to trigger a secondary or right-click style action where supported.

title: What makes BrowsePad different from a normal TV browser?
content: BrowsePad combines several TV-focused features that make web browsing easier on a television: a visible app-wide pointer overlay, smooth switching between pointer mode and toolbar focus, multi-tab browsing, desktop-style browsing controls, phone-based trackpad control, built-in remote text entry, bookmarks, history, downloads, and per-site view-mode options.

title: Does BrowsePad support multiple tabs?
content: Yes. BrowsePad supports multi-tab browsing with tab creation, tab switching, tab closing, and a close-all-tabs action. Each tab shows its own title and favicon, making it easier to recognize and return to the pages you have open.

title: Can websites open content in a new tab?
content: Yes. If a website requests a new browser window, BrowsePad handles that by opening the content in a new tab instead of ignoring it. This helps improve compatibility with websites that rely on popup or new-window behavior.

title: What can I type into the address bar?
content: The address bar accepts full URLs, bare domain names, and normal search queries. BrowsePad automatically decides whether your input should open as a website address or be sent to your selected search engine, so you do not need to format everything manually.

title: Which search engines are supported?
content: BrowsePad includes configurable search engine choices for Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Brave Search, Perplexity, and Andi. You can choose your preferred default search engine from the Settings page.

title: What is BrowsePad Connect?
content: BrowsePad Connect is the app's built-in phone pairing and remote-control system. It opens as a native Connect page inside the browser and allows you to pair a phone with your TV browser over the local network, turning your phone into a trackpad-style controller for browsing.

title: How do I connect my phone to BrowsePad?
content: Open the BrowsePad Connect page in the app. BrowsePad shows a QR code for fast pairing and also displays a local controller URL for manual connection. Once your phone opens that controller page on the same local network, it can connect to the TV browser and start controlling the pointer and text input.

title: Do my phone and TV need to be on the same Wi-Fi network?
content: Yes. BrowsePad Connect works over your local network, so the TV device running BrowsePad and the phone acting as the controller must be able to reach each other on the same network. This allows the controller page to communicate with the TV app in real time.

title: What can I do from the phone remote?
content: The phone controller supports single-finger cursor movement, tap-to-click, two-finger tap for right-click, two-finger scrolling, fling or momentum scrolling, pinch-to-zoom, and remote text input. It is designed to make web browsing on TV feel much closer to using a laptop trackpad.

title: Can I adjust the pointer speed on my phone?
content: Yes. The mobile controller includes pointer-speed options such as slow, normal, and fast. BrowsePad also remembers your phone-side pointer-speed preference, so you do not need to set it again every time you reconnect.

title: What happens if the phone remote disconnects?
content: BrowsePad Connect includes automatic reconnect behavior, heartbeat-based keepalive handling, and on-device diagnostics for connection events and failures. The Connect page can also show connection status and a last-disconnect summary when helpful, making it easier to understand what happened if the controller drops.

title: Can I enter text from my phone instead of using the TV keyboard?
content: Yes. BrowsePad supports remote text input from the phone. When the TV browser needs text input, the phone can show a text-entry panel for the address bar, webpage input fields, textareas, and content-editable fields. It supports live typing updates, submission, blur or dismiss behavior, and Enter-key submission.

title: How does the pointer work on TV?
content: BrowsePad includes an app-wide visible pointer overlay that works across browser controls, internal app pages, and webpage content. You can nudge it precisely with the TV remote, hold direction keys for continuous movement, and use edge scrolling when the pointer reaches the boundary of scrollable webpage content.

title: Can I still use D-pad focus navigation instead of the pointer?
content: Yes. BrowsePad is built to support both pointer-based and focus-based navigation. It provides a smooth handoff between pointer mode and toolbar focus mode, so you can move through browser chrome with D-pad focus and then return to pointer control when you want more precise interaction inside a webpage.

title: Does the browser interface get out of the way while browsing?
content: Yes. The browser chrome can auto-hide while you are viewing web content and reappear when needed. This gives you more screen space for the page itself while still keeping browser controls accessible.

title: Can I save bookmarks?
content: Yes. You can save or remove the current page using the browser toolbar bookmark button. BrowsePad also includes a dedicated Bookmarks page inside the browser where saved items are listed with their titles and cached favicons.

title: How are bookmarks organized?
content: Bookmarks are shown in a dedicated in-app Bookmarks page and are ordered newest first. Each saved entry can display the page title along with a cached favicon, making the list easier to scan and recognize.

title: Does BrowsePad save browsing history?
content: Yes. BrowsePad automatically records browsing history for regular web pages and provides a dedicated History page inside the browser. History entries are ordered newest first, so your most recent pages appear at the top.

title: Can I clear my browsing history?
content: Yes. BrowsePad includes a Clear History action with a confirmation step. This lets you remove recorded browsing history intentionally without having to delete items one by one.

title: Will my tabs come back after I restart the app?
content: Yes, for previously open non-internal tabs. BrowsePad supports session restore so that your open website tabs can be restored after relaunch, helping you continue where you left off.

title: What happens to internal pages like Connect or Help after restart?
content: BrowsePad's session restore is intended for previously open non-internal tabs. Internal app routes and utility pages are handled separately, so the restore behavior focuses on normal browsing tabs rather than treating every internal page like a regular website.

title: Does BrowsePad support desktop mode?
content: Yes. BrowsePad supports per-site view mode switching between Adaptive and Desktop. Desktop mode uses a desktop-style browser user agent, and you can also set a default view mode in Settings for sites that do not already have their own saved override.

title: Can I change the default search engine and view mode later?
content: Yes. The Settings page includes controls for changing the default search engine and the default site view mode. These settings let you tune the browsing experience without affecting your bookmarks or history.

title: Can websites show a context menu in BrowsePad?
content: Yes. BrowsePad supports web context menus on webpage content. Available context actions include back, forward, reload, open link in a new tab, copy a link URL, and download a linked or embedded resource when supported.

title: Can I download files from websites?
content: Yes. BrowsePad supports file downloads using Android's DownloadManager. Downloads are intercepted with a confirmation step before they begin, and the app provides feedback when a download starts and when it completes.

title: Where are downloaded files saved?
content: BrowsePad saves downloads to the public Downloads folder where the Android version and permissions allow it. If legacy write permission is unavailable, the app falls back to an app-specific external downloads location so downloads can still be stored reliably.

title: Can I download media or links from a context menu?
content: Yes. BrowsePad supports context-menu download actions for links and media resources, which makes it easier to save files directly from webpage content without manually opening them first.

title: What web technologies does BrowsePad support?
content: BrowsePad uses Android WebView with support for JavaScript, DOM storage, zoom controls, overview mode, wide viewport handling, and multiple windows. This gives modern websites the core browser features they typically expect on Android TV.

title: How does BrowsePad handle page errors or broken websites?
content: BrowsePad includes a friendly internal error page for page-load failures and handles common web issues such as host lookup failures, connection problems, timeouts, SSL failures, redirect loops, I/O problems, and unsupported URL schemes. This helps users understand why a page did not load instead of failing silently.

title: Does BrowsePad ignore unsafe SSL certificate problems?
content: No. BrowsePad uses safe SSL handling and blocks insecure certificate flows instead of bypassing them. This helps protect users from unsafe or misconfigured HTTPS connections.

title: Can I clear cached web data without deleting everything else?
content: Yes. BrowsePad includes a clear-cache flow in Settings that removes cached web data and saved favicons without deleting your bookmarks or browsing history. A confirmation flow is included so the action is not triggered accidentally.

title: What pages are available in the main app menu?
content: BrowsePad's app menu includes access to New Tab, Close All Tabs, Bookmarks, History, Subscription Management, Settings, Support and Feedback, Privacy Policy, Terms of Use, Help or FAQ, About, and Exit. Informational pages such as Support, Privacy Policy, Terms of Use, Help, and About open as web pages in a new browser tab.

title: Is there an exit confirmation before closing the app?
content: Yes. BrowsePad shows an exit confirmation dialog before closing the app and returning you to the TV home screen, which helps prevent accidental exits during browsing.

title: Does BrowsePad support subscriptions?
content: Yes. BrowsePad includes subscription support with separate store-specific purchase flows depending on where you installed the app. The app provides a subscription plans screen for starting a purchase and a subscription management entry point from the app menu.

title: Which app stores and billing systems are supported?
content: BrowsePad supports separate builds for Google Play, Amazon Appstore, and Huawei AppGallery. It includes store-specific subscription integrations for Google Play Billing, Amazon billing, and Huawei IAP, along with store-appropriate subscription management destinations for each build.

title: Will my subscription still work if I am temporarily offline?
content: Yes. BrowsePad uses cached subscription-status handling with an offline grace period after recent verification. That means the app can continue recognizing your subscription for a period of time even when the device is temporarily offline.

title: How do I manage my subscription?
content: You can open Subscription Management from the app menu. BrowsePad routes users to the appropriate store-specific management destination based on whether they are using the Google Play, Amazon, or Huawei version of the app.

title: Does the Huawei version have anything extra?
content: Yes. The Huawei build includes in-app update-check integration in addition to Huawei-specific subscription support, helping users on that store stay aware of app updates.

title: Does BrowsePad need internet access to browse websites?
content: Yes. BrowsePad is a web browser, so an internet connection is required to access online websites and services. However, some internal pages and app-managed UI experiences, such as the built-in Connect page and internal error page, are rendered from app assets and do not depend on a live website response.

title: Why does BrowsePad open the Connect page on a new tab?
content: BrowsePad's new-tab flow opens BrowsePad Connect by default so users can quickly pair a phone and start controlling the browser with a trackpad-style remote. This makes the mobile control system easy to discover right from the start.

title: Can BrowsePad be used without a phone at all?
content: Yes. The phone controller is optional. BrowsePad is designed to work with a regular TV remote alone, and the phone-based controller is there as an additional convenience for easier pointing, scrolling, zooming, and text entry.

title: How does BrowsePad store favicons?
content: BrowsePad uses persistent favicon caching in memory and on disk so site icons can be reused across tabs, bookmarks, and history. This helps pages and saved items remain visually recognizable even after restarting the app.

title: Can I see the app version inside BrowsePad?
content: Yes. The Settings page includes an app-version display so you can check which version of BrowsePad is currently installed on your device.
